metadata.purpose: Gold-label benchmark set for XFI-017 triage prompt validation and Phase 2 drift detection
metadata.notes: Deliberately includes off-topic bookmarks (health, politics, spiritual) to stress-test triage ignore/low-priority classification. Search results include both high-signal and noise examples.

labeled_posts.author: code_rams
labeled_posts.text:
Day 5 of debugging Chiti's memory.

Today wasn't about retrieval or indexing. 
It was about bloat.

I ran /context detail(telegram) and realized 
my agent was loading 11,887 tokens of system prompt on every new session.

51 skills. 20 of them I never used. 
200 lines of https://t.co/A9TjsQMAfy https://t.co/zTOvVYHDZh
labeled_posts.rationale: Direct parallel to Crumb context management — debugging agent memory bloat, /context detail usage

labeled_posts.author: intellectronica
labeled_posts.text:
A few notes on agentic @obsdmd (about a week in):

- This is amazing. So fast, so flexible.
- Bases for everything. All my notes have a `type` and matching template and structure. Bases organise them, the agent knows how to create, query, and update them.
- All notes are https://t.co/PvpBjXWFP3
labeled_posts.rationale: Agentic Obsidian with typed notes and bases — directly relevant to vault architecture and file conventions

labeled_posts.author: GanimCorey
labeled_posts.text:
The secret file he didn't mention:

`. learnings/LEARNINGS. md`

Every time my agent makes a mistake, it logs the correction and updates its own rules.

43 skills. 661 lines of learnings. An agent that gets smarter every day.

Your setup isn't just a moat. It's a flywheel. https://t.co/pcbjUKrsa3
labeled_posts.rationale: Agent mistake logging with LEARNINGS.md — direct compound engineering parallel

labeled_posts.author: zarazhangrui

labeled_posts.author: SystematicPeter
labeled_posts.text:

I've been experimenting with Claude Code as an autonomous research assistant for futures strategy development. Not a "let AI trade for you" pitch - just a structured workflow that runs overnight while I sleep. https://t.co/rrteqUOMCJ

labeled_posts.author: kathyyliao
labeled_posts.text:
~80% reduction in token usage for agents just by adding this header
-H "Accept: text/markdown"

And for accessing websites not on Cloudflare, you can always use the Browser Rendering /markdown REST API to render a dynamic page or application in a real browser before converting https://t.co/rpIY3ValSt
labeled_posts.rationale: 80% token reduction via Accept header — practical operational improvement for agent web access

labeled_posts.author: GoogleResearch
labeled_posts.text:
A common heuristic in LLM agent design—"more agents is better"—might be wrong.

Across 180 configurations, we find multi-agent coordination is task-contingent: +81% on parallelizable tasks (finance), but -70% on sequential ones (planning). Architecture-task alignment matters more than agent count.
labeled_posts.rationale: Google Research: multi-agent coordination is task-contingent, not universally better — directly informs Crumb architecture decisions

labeled_posts.author: TechAI_X
labeled_posts.text:
You don't need to burn $100s in token usage every month.

Instead, you can already connect Claude MCP to your apps and talk to them directly.

Here's how I turned a Linear issue list into a live presentation and shared it with my team in under 5 minutes:

First, make sure you have these connectors set up in Claude.

Linear for project management. Gamma for presentations. Slack for team communication.

Takes 30 seconds each.

Step 1: Pull your project data from Linear.

I asked Claude to get all subissues from our Migration to Multi-Agent Architecture project.

It exported 23 of them with their statuses. 9 done, 14 to do.

Step 2: Turn that data into a presentation.

I told Claude to create a project overview deck using Gamma.

It organized everything into 8 slides. Project summary, progress stats, and all 7 phases from foundation to production rollout.

Step 3: Share it with your team instantly.

I asked Claude to push the live presentation link to our shipped-today channel in Slack.

One prompt and the whole team had access.

No exporting CSVs. No copying links between apps.

Linear to Gamma to Slack. All from one conversation.

This is what AI agents actually look like for developers.

Not complex frameworks that need debugging. Just tools that connect and execute.

Want to try this yourself?

Connect Linear, Gamma, and Slack to Claude. Start automating your project updates today.

Start here: https://t.co/HHqQ2rt53M
labeled_posts.rationale: Engagement-bait tweet about MCP — generic advice, no substantive content or novel pattern

labeled_posts.author: AlexEngineerAI
labeled_posts.text:
AI automation creates a dangerous productivity flywheel.

I've been shipping 5x faster with AI over the last few months. The speed boost is incredible and I'm finally getting a handle on the workflow.

But the mental fatigue is real.

When you're self-employed, it's impossible to draw the line at the end of the day. Every feature is just a few prompts away from completion.

Don't get me wrong, I already worked too much before AI, but now it's so easy to say "just one more prompt" and boom it's 2AM.

The real competitive advantage doesn't go to whoever ships fastest. It goes to builders who master execution velocity without burning out.

Discipline is the new technical skill.
labeled_posts.rationale: Generic AI productivity take — no specific pattern, tool, or architectural insight to extract

labeled_posts.author: HeyAbhishek
